front: Poem: addressed to a "fair flower, that dost so comely grow"; "The space between is but an hour, / The frail duration of a flower"
Poem: opens "In spite of all the learned have said"; "the hunter still the deer pursues" at a Native American graveyard where corpses are seated upright
Poem: ends "Reason's self shall bow the knee / To shadows and delusions here" — site shows "fancies of a ruder race"
Poem: inspired by imprisonment on HMS Jersey; describes a "slaughter-house, yet hospital in name"
Author: hired by Thomas Jefferson as an anti-Federalist journalist; known as the "Poet of the American Revolution"
